title: Arc measure
description: Use the fact that the arc measures of a circle must sum to 360 degrees and other properties of angles that share a vertex to find missing arc measures.
topic_path: Module 5: Circles with and without coordinates
Topic B: Lessons  7-8: Angles and arcs

assessment_items.question_content:
In the figure below, $\overline{BD}$ and $\overline{EC}$ are diameters of circle $P$.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AE}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AE}$ is the measure of $\angle APE$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle APE$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Because $\overline{BD}$ is a diameter, we know that it forms a straight angle.  Thus $\angle DPE$, $\angle APE$, and $\angle APB$ are supplementary, and their measures add up to $180^\circ$.

$m\angle APE =180^\circ-63^\circ-90^\circ=27^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AE}$ is $27^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, C, D, and E all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segment B D and line segment E C are diameters of the circle. Line segment A P is a radius of the circle. The angle A P B is a right angle. The angle E P D is sixty-three degrees.

answers.message: When we name an arc using only 2 points, we are referring to the minor arc, that is the arc that is no more than 180 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AB}$ on circle $P$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AB}$ is the measure of $\angle APB$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle APB$.



[[☃ image 1]]

There are $360^\circ$ in a circle, so the measure of any minor arc is equal to $360^\circ$ minus the measure of the corresponding major arc.

$m\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AB} \,= 360^\circ-286^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AB}$ is $74^\circ$.

answers.message: This is the measure of the major arc.  What is the measure of the reflex angle going the rest of the way around the circle?
The central angles in a circle sum to 360, not 180 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the figure below, $\overline{BD}$ and $\overline{AC}$ are diameters of circle $P$.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{DC}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{DC}$ is the measure of $\angle DPC$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle DPC$.



[[☃ image 1]]
The measures of $\angle DPC$ and $\angle APB$ are congruent because they are vertical angles.
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{DC}$ is $19^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, C, and D all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segment B D and line segment A C are diameters of the circle. Line segments A P, B P, C P, and D P are radii of the circle. The angle A P B is a nineteen degree angle.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the figure below, $\overline{AD}$ and $\overline{CE}$ are diameters of circle $P$.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AB}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AB}$ is the measure of $\angle APB$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle APB$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The measures of $\angle DPE$ and $\angle APC$ are congruent because they are vertical angles.

$m\angle APC = 93^\circ$

The measures of $\angle APB$ and $\angle BPC$ add up to the measure of $\angle APC$.

$m\angle APB+38^\circ = 93^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AB}$ is $55^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, C, D, and E all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segment A D and line segment C E are diameters of the circle. Line segments A P, B P, C P, D P, and E P are radii of the circle. The angle B P C is a thirty-eight degree angle. The angle D P E is a ninety-three degree angle.

answers.message: Angle APC is not a right angle.  Try using a vertical angle relationship to solve for the arc measure.

assessment_items.question_content:
**What is the arc measure, in degrees, of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ on circle $P$ below?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ is the measure of $\angle APC$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle APC$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The measure of $\angle APC$ is equal to the sums of the measures of $\angle APB$ and $\angle BPC$.

$m\angle APC =70^\circ+104^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ is $174^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, and C all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segments A P, B P, and C P are radii of the circle. The angle A P B is a seventy degree angle. The angle B P C is a one hundred four degree angle.

answers.message: When we name an arc using only 2 points, we are referring to the minor arc, that is the arc that is no more than 180 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the figure below, $\overline{AB}$ is a diameter of circle $P$.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ on circle $P$ in degrees?**


[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ is the measure of $\angle APC$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle APC$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Segment $\overline{AB}$ is a diameter, so $\angle APC$ and $\angle CPB$ are supplementary.

$\begin{align}
m\angle APC + m\angle CPB &= 180^\circ \\\\
m\angle APC + 41^\circ &= 180^\circ \\\\
m\angle APC &= 180-41^\circ
\end{align}$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ is $139^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, and C all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segment A B is a diameter. Line segments A P, B P, and C P are radii of the circle. The angle B P C is a forty-one degree angle.

answers.message: When we name an arc using only 2 points, we are referring to the minor arc, that is the arc that is no more than 180 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the figure below, $\angle APE$ and $\angle EPD$ are congruent.

**What is the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ on circle $P$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

The arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ is the measure of $\angle APC$. We need to find the measure of  $\angle APC$.


[[☃ image 1]]

The measures of central angles in a circle add up to $360^\circ$.

$m\angle APC + 136+74= 360^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ is $150^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, C, D, and E all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segments A P, B P, C P, D P, and E P are radii of the circle. The angle A P B is a one hundred thirty-six degree angle. The angle B P C is a seventy-four degree angle. The angle C P D is a forty-two degree angle.  Angle A P E and D P E are congruent.

answers.message: When we name an arc using only 2 points, we are referring to the minor arc, that is the arc that is no more than 180 degrees.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the figure below, $\overline{BD}$ and $\overline{EC}$ are diameters of circle $P$.

**What is the arc measure of major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{ADE}$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

By that reasoning, the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AE}$ is the acute measure of $\angle APE$. We need to find the measure of major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{ADE}$, which is the [[☃ definition 2]] of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AE}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

Because $\overline{BD}$ is a diameter, we know that it forms a straight angle.  Thus $\angle DPE$, $\angle APE$, and $\angle APB$ are supplementary, and their measures add up to $180^\circ$.

$m\angle APE =180^\circ-63^\circ-90^\circ=27^\circ$

There are $360^\circ$ in a circle, so the measure of any major arc is equal to $360^\circ$ minus the measure of the corresponding minor arc.

$m\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{ADE} \,= 360^\circ-27^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{ADE}$ is $333^\circ$.

options.alt: A circle that is centered around point P. Points A, B, C, D, and E all lie on this circle in a clockwise direction. Line segment B D and line segment E C are diameters of the circle. Line segment A P is a radius of the circle. The angle A P B is a right angle. The angle E P D is sixty-three degrees.

answers.message: This is the measure of the minor arc.  What is the measure of the reflex angle going the rest of the way around the circle?

answers.message: This is the measure of the minor arc.  What is the measure of the reflex angle going the rest of the way around the circle?
The central angles in a circle sum to 360, not 180 degrees.

hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

By that reasoning, the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ is the acute measure of $\angle APC$. We need to find the measure of major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{ADC}$, which is the [[☃ definition 2]] of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$.

$m\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{ADC} \,= 360^\circ-174^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{ADC}$ is $186^\circ$.

The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

By that reasoning, the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$ is the acute measure of $\angle APC$. We need to find the measure of major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{ABC}$, which is the [[☃ definition 2]] of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{AC}$.

m\angle APC &=180^\circ-69^\circ\\\\

\end{align}$

There are $360^\circ$ in a circle, so the measure of any major arc is equal to $360^\circ$ minus the measure of the corresponding minor arc.

assessment_items.question_content:
In the figure below, $\angle APE$ and $\angle EPD$ are congruent.

**What is the arc measure of major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BAD}$ on circle $P$ in degrees?**



[[☃ image 1]]

[[☃ numeric-input 1]]$^\circ$
hints:
The measure of an arc is equal to the measure of the [[☃ definition 1]] that intercepts it.

By that reasoning, the arc measure of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BD}$ is the acute measure of $\angle BPD$.  We need to find the measure of major arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BAD}$, which is the [[☃ definition 2]] of minor arc $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BD}$.



[[☃ image 1]]

The measure of $\angle BPD$ is equal to the sums of the measures of $\angle BPC$ and $\angle CPD$.

$\begin{align}
m\angle BPD &=74^\circ+42^\circ\\\\
&= 116^\circ
\end{align}$

There are $360^\circ$ in a circle, so the measure of any major arc is equal to $360^\circ$ minus the measure of the corresponding minor arc.

$m\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BAD} \,= 360^\circ-116^\circ$
The arc measure of $\stackrel{\large{\frown}}{BAD}$ is $244^\circ$.
